headline: LITE files 10-Q: periodic report
quote: The increase in our Systems products was primarily driven by our cloud transceiver product lines which increased by more than $137.0 million due to higher shipment volume, partially offset by lower average selling prices. We also continued the initial phase of optical circuit switch shipments, which contributed more than $25.0 million in revenue during the three months ended March 28, 2026.

summary.tldr: FQ3 26 revenue $808.4M +90.1% YoY with cloud transceivers +$137M and laser chips ramping at 200G lane; gross margin 44.2% (+1,540bps) as 1.6T cycle structurally re-rates margins.
takeaways: Q3 FY26 revenue $808.4M +90.1% YoY (+$383M); Components $533.3M +77.3%; Systems $275.1M +121.1%; 9M revenue $2.01B +72.4%
Gross margin 44.2% in Q (+1,540bps YoY); 9M gross margin 38.8% (+1,300bps); operating leverage from supply allocation + 200G-lane mix
Systems growth driven by cloud transceivers +$137M (higher volume offset by lower ASPs); optical circuit switches contributed >$25M in Q, >$38M 9M
Laser chip ramp continues to drive Components, with 200G lane-speed mix lifting ASPs — confirms LITE is the InP/EML supply choke point for 1.6T transceivers
Customer concentration deepened: 2 customers at 26% and 12% in Q (combined 38%) — top customer share rose from 24%
Mgmt language hardened: 'demand is outpacing current supply which has required us to make decisions on supply allocation' (vs 'has led to' previous Q)
summary.quote: The increase in our Systems products was primarily driven by our cloud transceiver product lines which increased by more than $137.0 million due to higher shipment volume, partially offset by lower average selling prices. summary.tldr: FY26 revenue $4.7B (vs $3.6B); RPO $9.8B (46% to recognize next 12 months); 13,328 customers (+21%); 733 $1M+ customers vs 576; NRR 125%.
takeaways: FY26 revenue $4.7B vs $3.6B FY25 (+31%); product revenue $4.5B implied
Total customers 13,328 (+21% from 10,996); customers with >$1M trailing product revenue jumped to 733 from 576 (+27%)
790 Forbes Global 2000 customers contributed ~43% of revenue
RPO $9.8B at Jan 31 2026 (+24% from $7.9B at Oct 31 2025); 46% to recognize next 12 months
Net revenue retention rate 125% (vs 126%); weighted-avg contract life 2.7 years
Net loss $1.3B for fiscal 2026 (stock-based comp ~34% of revenue, down from 41%)
Operating cash flow $1.2B for FY26; deferred revenue +$755M; began ASC 985-20 self-hosted accounting for public sector

guidance.notes: 10-K provides no explicit guidance; expects NRR to decline long-term as install base matures

summary.quote: As of January 31, 2026, our remaining performance obligations were approximately $9.8 billion, of which we expect approximately 46% to be recognized as revenue in the 12 months ending January 31, 2027 based on historical customer consumption patterns.

headline: MU files 10-Q: periodic report
quote: Sales of DRAM products increased 207%, primarily due to a mid-110% range increase in average selling prices and a mid-40% range increase in bit shipments. Sales of NAND products increased 169%, primarily due to a slightly more than 100% increase in average selling prices.

summary.tldr: Q2 FY26 revenue $23.9B (+196% YoY, +75% QoQ); gross margin 74%, net income $13.8B as DRAM ASPs jumped mid-110% YoY on AI-driven supply shortage.
takeaways: Q2 FY26 revenue $23.86B (+75% QoQ, +196% YoY); CMBU $7.75B, CDBU $5.69B, MCBU set higher, AEBU growth across the board
DRAM ASPs +mid-60% QoQ, +mid-110% YoY; NAND ASPs +high-70% QoQ, +slightly more than 100% YoY
Consolidated gross margin 74% (vs 56% in Q1 FY26, 37% in Q2 FY25); operating margin 68%; net income $13.8B
One customer = 13% of six-month revenue (primarily CMBU, i.e. HBM/hyperscale)
Tight industry supply and favorable mix driving both DRAM and NAND price improvements; supply allocation continues
Effective tax rate rose to 13.7% from prior periods due to Singapore Pillar Two implementation

guidance.notes: Industry supply allocation decisions continue to impact certain customers as memory/storage demand exceeds overall industry supply

summary.quote: Sales of DRAM products increased 207%, primarily due to a mid-110% range increase in average selling prices and a mid-40% range increase in bit shipments. Sales of NAND products increased 169%, primarily due to a slightly more than 100% increase in average selling prices.

headline: MU files 8-K: Earnings release
quote: Micron set new records across revenue, gross margin, EPS, and free cash flow in fiscal Q2, driven by a strong demand environment, tight industry supply, and our strong execution, and we expect significant records again in fiscal Q3.

summary.tldr: Q2 FY26 blowout: revenue $23.86B (+196% YoY, +75% QoQ) crushed the $18.7B guide by 28%; non-GAAP EPS $12.20 vs $8.42 guide; Q3 guides to $33.5B and ~81% GM with EPS $19.15.
takeaways: Q2 FY26 reported: revenue $23.86B (+196% YoY, +75% QoQ) — beat Q2 guide of $18.7B by 28%; non-GAAP GM 74.9% (vs 68% guide), non-GAAP EPS $12.20 (vs $8.42 guide, +45% beat)
DRAM ASPs +mid-110% YoY / +mid-60% QoQ; NAND ASPs +100%+ YoY / +high-70% QoQ — AI-driven supply shortage drove memory pricing higher
CMBU revenue $7.75B at 66% op margin; CDBU $5.69B at 67%; MCBU $7.71B at 76%; AEBU $2.71B at 62% — every BU at record op margin
Q3 FY26 guidance: revenue $33.5B ± $750M (+40% QoQ implied), non-GAAP GM ~81%, non-GAAP opex ~$1.40B, non-GAAP EPS $19.15 ± $0.40
Dividend raised 30% to $0.15/share — first hike in years; signals management confidence cycle isn't immediately rolling
Ending cash/investments $16.7B (+$4.7B QoQ); operating cash flow $11.9B; adjusted FCF $6.9B

guidance.notes: Diluted EPS $19.15 ± $0.40 non-GAAP ($18.90 ± $0.40 GAAP); dividend raised 30%; CEO: 'we expect significant records again in fiscal Q3'

summary.quote: Micron set new records across revenue, gross margin, EPS, and free cash flow in fiscal Q2, driven by a strong demand environment, tight industry supply, and our strong execution, and we expect significant records again in fiscal Q3.
summary.quote_section: Q2 FY26 earnings release (CEO Sanjay Mehrotra)

headline: OKLO files 10-K: periodic report
quote: On January 5, 2026, we entered into a prepayment agreement (the "Prepayment Agreement") with Meta Platforms, Inc. ("Meta") that advances plans to develop a 1.2 gigawatt power campus in Pike County, Ohio, to support Meta's data centers. The Prepayment Agreement provides a mechanism for Meta to prepay for power and provide funding to advance powerhouse deployment.

summary.tldr: Jan 5, 2026 prepayment agreement with Meta for 1.2 GW Pike County, Ohio power campus; FY25 opex $139.3M; targets 2026 op cash burn $80-100M, capex $350-450M.
takeaways: Jan 5, 2026: prepayment agreement with Meta for development of 1.2 GW power campus in Pike County, Ohio to support Meta data centers — first hyperscaler prepayment for advanced fission
FY25 operating expenses $139.3M vs $52.8M (+164%); accumulated deficit $199.3M
Dec 2025: raised $300M via ATM at $88.29 avg (closed Jan 28, 2026 after raising remaining of $1.5B program); also acquired Pike County Ohio land
Feb 2026: Aurora Nuclear Safety Design Agreement approved by DOE; first commercial Aurora powerhouse deployment target remains 2028
12 GW Master Power Agreement with Switch signed Dec 2024 still in place; LOIs with Equinix, Diamondback, Prometheus Hyperscale active
Tennessee Advanced Fuel Center: roadmap of up to $1.68B investment for first US commercial fuel recycling facility, partnering with Kiewit as lead constructor
205 full-time employees at YE25 incl 13 PhDs, 50 master's degrees, 8 former NRC staff
Pursuing alternative fuel materials including DOE plutonium for dilute-and-dispose programs

guidance.notes: Expect total cash used in operating expenses 2026 in $80-100M range; total cash used in investing activities $350-450M

summary.quote: On January 5, 2026, we entered into a prepayment agreement (the "Prepayment Agreement") with Meta Platforms, Inc. ("Meta") that advances plans to develop a 1.2 gigawatt power campus in Pike County, Ohio, to support Meta's data centers. The Prepayment Agreement provides a mechanism for Meta to prepay for power and provide funding to advance powerhouse deployment.

headline: NVIDIA invests $4B combined into Lumentum + Coherent to lock 200G EML supply
quote: Strategic investment ($2B into LITE at $695.31/sh, plus Coherent stake) to lock priority on 200G/lane EMLs for 1.6T transceivers. Confirms EML lasers as the textbook 2026 bottleneck.

headline: AVGO files 10-Q: periodic report
quote: We believe aggregate sales to our top five end customers, through all channels, accounted for approximately 50% and 40% of our net revenue for the fiscal quarters ended February 1, 2026 and February 2, 2025, respectively.

summary.tldr: Q1 FY26 revenue $19.3B (+29% YoY) with Semiconductor solutions $12.5B (+52%); customer concentration intensifying - top five end customers now ~50% of revenue (up from 40%) and one distributor 42%.
takeaways: Q1 FY26 revenue $19.3B (+29% YoY); Semiconductor solutions $12.5B (+52%) on custom AI accelerators and AI networking; Infrastructure software $6.8B (+1%)
Top five end customers ~50% of Q1 FY26 revenue, up sharply from ~40% a year ago - AI XPU concentration tightening
One semi distributor customer = 42% of total revenue this quarter (vs 29% prior-year Q1) - implies one hyperscaler XPU customer effectively driving the print
Gross margin flat at 68% YoY despite higher semi mix; semi gross margin benefit offset by mix shift
Operating income $8.6B (44% margin) vs $6.3B (42%); R&D +32% to $3.0B on higher stock-comp from Two-Year Equity Awards
Semi solutions now 65% of revenue (vs 55% prior-year Q1) - AI semi ramp shifting mix away from software
summary.quote: We believe aggregate sales to our top five end customers, through all channels, accounted for approximately 50% and 40% of our net revenue for the fiscal quarters ended February 1, 2026 and February 2, 2025, respectively.

headline: MRVL files 10-K: FORM 10-K
quote: Our net revenue for fiscal 2026 increased by $2.4 billion compared to net revenue for fiscal 2025. This was primarily due to a 46% increase in sales from the data center end market which benefited from strong AI-related demand.

summary.tldr: FY26 revenue $8.2B (+42% YoY); Data center end-market $6.1B (74% of mix, +46% YoY) on AI custom ASICs and electro-optics; closed Celestial AI and XConn acquisitions post year-end to extend photonic fabric and CXL/PCIe switching.
takeaways: FY26 revenue $8.2B (+42% YoY); Data center $6.1B (74% of mix, +46% YoY); Communications & other $2.1B (+31%) recovering from inventory correction
Gross margin 51.0% vs 41.3% prior year (+9.7 pts) - prior year had $358M of restructuring impairments; cost absorption also improved on higher revenue
Customer concentration: Direct Customer A = 14% of revenue (vs 13%), Distributor A = 37% (vs 34%); top 10 customers historically ~81% of revenue
Post-year-end: closed Celestial AI (Feb 2, 2026) for ~$1.3B cash + 24.5M shares for Photonic Fabric scale-up interconnect; closed XConn (Feb 10, 2026) for $280M + 2.1M shares for PCIe/CXL switching

Beginning Q4 FY26, MRVL consolidated enterprise networking, carrier, consumer, and auto/industrial into single 'Communications and other' end market - data center now the only standalone segment disclosed
Roadmap explicit: custom AI ASIC platform leverages SerDes, ARM compute, silicon photonics, CPO, custom HBM; 5nm in production, 3nm in design, 2nm/1.4nm in development

guidance.notes: Expects U.S. export restrictions on certain Chinese customers to continue impacting revenue; flagged risk that Chinese customers may stockpile or move to indigenous suppliers

summary.quote: Our net revenue for fiscal 2026 increased by $2.4 billion compared to net revenue for fiscal 2025. This was primarily due to a 46% increase in sales from the data center end market which benefited from strong AI-related demand.

headline: ORCL files 10-Q: periodic report
quote: Remaining performance obligations were $552.6 billion and $130.2 billion as of February 28, 2026 and 2025, respectively. The increase in remaining performance obligations... was primarily attributable to certain significant cloud contracts that were entered into during the period.

summary.tldr: Q3 FY26 OCI +84% to $4.89B; RPO $552.6B vs $130.2B YoY; 9-month capex $39.2B (3x prior year); issued $42.7B senior notes + $5B mandatory convert to fund buildout.
takeaways: Q3 FY26 (Feb 28 2026) total revenue $17.19B (+22% YoY); cloud and software $15.03B
Cloud infrastructure revenue $4.89B (+84%, +81% constant currency); cloud applications $4.03B (+13%)
RPO $552.6B at Feb 28 2026 vs $130.2B at Feb 28 2025 - 4.2x increase
9M FY26 capex $39.2B vs $12.1B 9M FY25 (3.2x); upward trend to continue 'next few fiscal years'
Issued $42.7B senior notes (Sept 2025 + Feb 2026) + $5B Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock + ATM offering program
9M operating margin $14.5B (+15%); margin % declined as infrastructure spend ramps

guidance.notes: Capex trend continues through FY26 and next few years; new ATM offering program initiated for additional equity issuance

summary.quote: Remaining performance obligations were $552.6 billion and $130.2 billion as of February 28, 2026 and 2025, respectively. The increase in remaining performance obligations... was primarily attributable to certain significant cloud contracts that were entered into during the period.

headline: HPE files 10-Q: periodic report
quote: Networking segment net revenue increased by $1,630 million, or 151.5%, primarily due to a $1,031 million, or 130.7%, increase in product revenue, and $599 million, or 208.7%, increase in service revenue. The increase in product revenue was primarily led by revenue attributable to Juniper Networks of $1,061 million, or 134.5%.

summary.tldr: Q1 FY26 revenue $9.3B +18.4% as Networking +151.5% YoY on full-quarter Juniper inclusion; Cloud & AI -2.7% on server AUP/volume decline despite AI-server growth.
takeaways: Q1 FY26 revenue $9.3B +18.4%; Networking +151.5% YoY (+$1.6B) to $2.7B as Juniper contributed full quarter (only 4 months in prior comparable)
Cloud & AI segment (new combined Server + Storage + Financial Services) -2.7% to $6.4B; AI servers growing but GPU-heavy mix carries 'limited margins'
Gross margin 35.9% (+670bps YoY) on Juniper revenue mix; non-GAAP operating margin 12.7% (+280bps)
Juniper integration costs $123M in quarter; restructuring/cost program added $23M; ongoing synergy capture
Segment-realignment effective 11/1/25: Server + Hybrid Cloud + Financial Services merged into 'Cloud & AI'; Telco/Instant On moved to Corporate Other (Instant On divested per DOJ)
Supreme Court 2/20/26 struck down IEEPA tariffs but new tariffs announced — net effect on margins still uncertain; memory component shortages flagged as new risk

guidance.notes: $600M Juniper synergies by FY28 with $800M investment reaffirmed; AI server growth flagged as margin-dilutive due to GPU pass-through pricing

summary.quote: Networking segment net revenue increased by $1,630 million, or 151.5%, primarily due to a $1,031 million, or 130.7%, increase in product revenue, and $599 million, or 208.7%, increase in service revenue. The increase in product revenue was primarily led by revenue attributable to Juniper Networks of $1,061 million, or 134.5%.

headline: Broadcom Q1 FY26: AI revenue $5.1B (+77% YoY), 5 hyperscaler designs
quote: AI revenue $5.1B (+77% YoY), 3 hyperscaler customers shipping, 2 more in design. Custom-AI ASIC share trajectory toward ~60% by 2027.

headline: >50% of US 2026 data centers expected delayed/cancelled — electrical gear shortage
quote: More than half of US 2026 data centers expected to be delayed or cancelled due to lack of transformers and electrical equipment. Lead times stretched from 24–30 months to 5 years.

headline: CRWV files 10-K: periodic report

headline: CRM files 10-K: periodic report

Current remaining performance obligation... was approximately $35.1 billion, an increase of 16 percent year-over-year.

Total RPO $72.4B (+14% YoY); current RPO $35.1B (+16%) - cRPO outpacing total RPO signals near-term demand acceleration

Renamed service offerings to Agentforce branding in Q3 FY26; Agentforce 360 Platform is the unified AI/CRM stack
Attrition rate ~8% as of Jan 31 2026, unchanged YoY

Current remaining performance obligation... was approximately $35.1 billion, an increase of 16 percent year-over-year.

headline: CLS files 10-K: periodic report
quote: HPS revenue for 2025 increased 81% (to $5.0 billion) compared to 2024, and accounted for 41% of our total 2025 revenue (compared to 29% of our total 2024 revenue), driven by accelerating volumes in our ramping networking switch programs with hyperscaler customers.

summary.tldr: FY25 revenue $12.2B with CCS +42% to $9.19B and HPS +81% to $5.0B (41% of total) on hyperscaler networking switch ramps; FY26 capex stepping up to ~$1B (6% of revenue, vs historical 1.5-2.0%).
takeaways: CCS segment +42% to $9.19B; Communications end-market +81% on data-center networking and switch program ramps
HPS revenue +81% to $5.0B, now 41% of total (vs 29% in 2024); Q4 HPS alone hit ~$1.4B (+72% YoY)
Customer concentration tightened: 3 CCS customers each over 10% of revenue (32%, 14%, 12%); top 10 = 79% of revenue
Enterprise end-market revenue -19% on a hyperscaler AI/ML compute program technology transition; Q4 already inflecting back with +33% on next-gen ramp
FY26 capex guided ~$1B (~6% of revenue) vs historical 1.5-2.0%, supporting hyperscaler capacity additions
Q4 gross margin 11.8% (vs 11.7% Q4 2024); CCS segment margin 8.4% in Q4 on improved operating leverage

guidance.notes: FY26 capex of approximately $1 billion (~6% of currently anticipated revenue) to support hyperscaler-driven demand; long-term margin profile expected to benefit from HPS mix

summary.quote: HPS revenue for 2025 increased 81% (to $5.0 billion) compared to 2024, and accounted for 41% of our total 2025 revenue (compared to 29% of our total 2024 revenue), driven by accelerating volumes in our ramping networking switch programs with hyperscaler customers.

headline: ETN files 10-K: periodic report

takeaways: Total backlog $19.8B at YE25, 69% targeted for delivery in next 12 months
Electrical Americas: sales $13.3B (+16%), backlog $13.2B (+31% YoY, +19% organic), book-to-bill 1.2x; data center strength was primary growth driver
Electrical Global: sales $6.8B (+9%), backlog $2.0B (+19%); data center strength in both segments
Nov 2025: signed agreement to acquire Boyd Thermal for $9.5B — liquid cooling + thermal solutions for hyperscale/colocation data centers (chip to grid)
2025 acquisitions: Fibrebond (modular DC power enclosures), Resilient (solid-state transformers for DC), Ultra PCS (aerospace)
Electrical Americas operating margin 29.9% (vs 30.2%) compressed by 380bps from commodity/wage inflation

guidance.notes: Boyd Thermal acquisition expected to close Q2 2026

Blackwell Ultra/GB300 shipped in Q2 FY26; Rubin next on annual cadence
Q1 FY26 $4.5B H20 inventory charge from China export controls; Feb 2026 H200 license carries 25% US import tariff
Mgmt explicitly flags data center, energy, and capital availability as constraints on customer deployments
$17.5B invested in private cos / infra funds + $3.5B in land/power/shell guarantees to early-stage AI customers
Gross margin compressed YoY due to Hopper HGX -> Blackwell full-system transition

headline: SNPS files 10-Q: periodic report
quote: Revenues were $2.4 billion, an increase of $953.5 million or 66%, which includes revenues from Ansys of $885.6 million. The remaining growth came organically across a majority of products and geographies, offset by weakness in our Design IP segment.

summary.tldr: Q1 FY26 revenue $2.41B +66% YoY ($885.6M from Ansys); Design Automation +96% but Design IP -6%; backlog steady at $11.3B; Processor IP business sold to GlobalFoundries.
takeaways: Q1 FY26 revenue $2,408.8M +66% YoY; Design Automation $2,001.8M +96% (Ansys contributed $885.6M); Design IP $407M -6% YoY
Backlog $11.3B at 1/31/26 (vs $11.4B at YE25) incl. $1.9B non-cancelable FSA; 47% to recognize in next 12mo
Operating income $203M (vs $251.8M prior) compressed by $394.1M Ansys intangible amortization; cost of revenue + opex +83%
Q1 restructuring charge $118.3M under '2026 Plan' (total $300-350M) for post-Ansys workforce reduction
Jan 14, 2026: signed agreement to divest Processor IP business to GlobalFoundries as part of Design IP segment reallocation toward highest-growth opportunities
Paid off remaining $3.5B term loan in Q1 FY26 (had been used to fund Ansys cash consideration); $13.5B FY25-end debt being aggressively paid down before buyback resumes

guidance.notes: Expect growth across most geographies in fiscal 2026; challenging near-term environment in China due to macro and Trade Restrictions; continue to expect muted Design IP segment growth in fiscal 2026

summary.quote: Revenues were $2.4 billion, an increase of $953.5 million or 66%, which includes revenues from Ansys of $885.6 million. The remaining growth came organically across a majority of products and geographies, offset by weakness in our Design IP segment.

headline: PWR files 10-K: periodic report
